config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is how abuse and injustice can quietly grow. A healthy society protects people, especially minorities and unpopular voices, instead of sacrificing them for some vague "common good." In my view, the right balance is to limit individual freedom only when there is a clear and necessary harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena di pengalaman saya di Amerika Serikat, justru hak individu sering menjadi pelindung utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi kalau dijadikan alasan utama tanpa batas yang jelas, minoritas, orang yang tidak populer, dan suara kritis bisa mudah dikorbankan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu demi “kebaikan bersama,” melainkan yang membatasi hak hanya ketika ada bahaya nyata bagi orang lain. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat atau individu secara mutlak, melainkan perlindungan hak dasar dengan pengecualian yang benar-benar sempit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “society first” can become a justification for silencing dissent, pressuring minorities, or excusing abuse by authorities. Society does matter, but it is not some higher moral entity that should automatically override individual rights; the whole point of rights is to protect people when the majority or the state gets it wrong. For me, public interest should only limit individual rights in narrow cases where there is clear harm to others, not just because something is seen as socially inconvenient or culturally unpopular.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, justru hak individu adalah pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” sering terdengar masuk akal, tetapi tanpa batas yang tegas, itu mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau mengorbankan orang yang tidak populer. Saya juga melihat poin bahwa masyarakat memang penting, tetapi kepentingan umum seharusnya hanya membatasi hak ketika ada bahaya nyata dan langsung bagi orang lain, bukan sekadar karena sesuatu dianggap tidak nyaman atau tidak sesuai selera mayoritas. Jadi bagi saya, prioritas yang benar adalah perlindungan hak dasar, dengan pengecualian yang sempit dan benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The statement is too broad, and from my Indonesian perspective I do not accept giving society automatic priority over the individual. In Indonesia, “for the public good” can easily be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse weak accountability, so I am cautious of any claim that puts collective interests above rights by default. Society does matter, but it is healthiest when it protects individual rights first and limits them only in narrow cases where there is clear harm to others. If we reverse that order, the people with less power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari pengalaman dan nilai yang kuat di Amerika Serikat, memberi prioritas otomatis pada masyarakat terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi kebaikan bersama; justru itu batas yang mencegah mayoritas atau negara mengorbankan orang yang lemah, tidak populer, atau kritis. Saya paham argumen bahwa masyarakat perlu ketertiban dan solidaritas, tetapi menurut saya pembatasan hak hanya layak jika ada bahaya nyata dan langsung bagi orang lain, bukan sekadar karena sesuatu dianggap lebih nyaman bagi mayoritas. Jika urutannya dibalik, yang paling sering rugi justru minoritas dan orang biasa yang tidak punya banyak perlindungan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
